methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
PILGRIM is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Jake Adler that has raised $4.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 19 same-sector peers, including two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private firms such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, and Shield AI.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($4.3M) as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to project post-seed valuation yields $10.75M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while 19 Defense & Security companies exist in the broader comp set, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier), making a narrow peer median unreliable. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given PILGRIM's specialized sector focus.

The triangulated valuation range is $9M to $12M, with a median of $11M, derived from the single applicable method. This represents a conservative band reflecting limited peer comparables at the seed stage in defense tech. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific sector, the absence of precision-calibrated peer benchmarks, and typical seed-stage execution risk. Further valuation refinement would require either comparable seed-stage transactions in defense tech or clarity on near-term funding trajectory.
